issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a background of #ffffff (var(--colour-bg-page)). This results in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and no visible alternative. This suppresses the focus ring on buttons, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility and may expose screen-reader-only text to all users or conversely fail to hide it properly.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who have requested reduced motion, potentially triggering vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0. This creates a touch target of 28×28 px, which is below the 24×24 px minimum and significantly below the 44×44 px recommended size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px with vertical padding of var(--space-1-5) (4.5px). Since the padding is less than 8px, the total height (45px) meets the minimum but the explicit min-height of 36px falls in the 24–43px range where additional padding is recommended to ensure a 44 px touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size. This makes the control difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited fine motor control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no compensating padding to reach the recommended 44 px touch target. While this meets the minimum 24 px requirement, the small target size may still be difficult for some users to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector defines a transition that includes transform (transform var(--transition-fast)), which creates spatial movement on state changes. No prefers-reduced-motion media query overrides this animation, failing to respect user motion preferences.

issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has transition: transform var(--transition-base), which animates spatial movement. There is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transition for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.2:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading hint text.
issuesFound.fix: Line .input-hint { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); } /* #475569 */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements (buttons, links, form controls) that rely on the default browser focus ring or this global rule, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which does not hide the content visually but keeps it in the layout flow. More critically, it fails to use the standard clip or absolute positioning technique, meaning content intended only for screen readers is visible to all users, breaking the visual layout. Conversely, if the intent was to hide from everyone, it fails to use display: none.
issuesFound.fix: Line .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn active state
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), and the .btn:active state applies transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion, which can cause vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px size: .btn

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative, removing focus indicators for high-contrast mode users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users with no alternative visual indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px exactly, meeting the minimum but falling below the 44px recommended size without padding compensation on the vertical axis.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1205: 
  ```css
  footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-offset: -3px but retains outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule, providing no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see when inset-focused elements receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, clip: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it visually. Conversely, it fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers as intended.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button

issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a barrier for users who require larger touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users may have difficulty selecting checkboxes accurately.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1153: 
  ```css
  .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }
  ```

Line 1205:

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards

Users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive links that rely solely on colour.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1303: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px. This meets the minimum but is below the recommended 44px size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1323: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have a min-height of 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1333: .social-links a { min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 423 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 423: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform: rotateY/rotateX and transition. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 718 is empty and does not disable this spatial movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card uses transform: scale/translate on hover. This rule overrides the generic media query rule due to specificity/order, re-enabling motion for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } (Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce))

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, which fails the 3:1 minimum for large text (18px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 773: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The input hint text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1293: .input-hint { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links use #94a3b8 on #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.5:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1323: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { color: var(--colour-neutral-200); ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. Most links rely solely on colour to distinguish them from text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 273: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline alternative to indicate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline entirely (outline: none) with only an offset, providing no visible focus indicator in high contrast mode. Users relying on forced colors cannot see where focus is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Content remains visible in the layout while being intended for screen readers only, causing visual clutter. Additionally, it lacks the necessary clip or clip-path properties to ensure content is accessible to assistive technology while hidden from view.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies transform: translateY(1px) without a corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582: .btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } and add at line 468: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image

issuesFound.fix: Line 835: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA buttons

issuesFound.fix: Add at line 900: .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } and .feature-card a:hover::after { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Many link contexts (navigation menus, account links, footer links) rely solely on color changes to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with color vision deficiencies cannot identify links without an additional visual cue such as underlines, borders, or font-weight changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 262: a { text-decoration: underline; } or for specific contexts add non-color differentiators such as: Line 687: #nav-menu > li > a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-info-light); text-decoration: underline; } Line 745: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; } Line 1437: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but omits aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from knowing their input contains errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1282: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the navigation drawer visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2042: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: characterCounter not associated with input via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: Creates a live region character counter but does not link it to the input field via aria-describedby. Screen reader users may not discover the character count feedback when typing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3112: Add `srUtils.addDescribedBy(field, counterEl);` after the counterEl is inserted into the DOM.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Assistive technologies cannot convey the open state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2157: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: Expands the FAQ answer panel without setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function sets it to false, the omission in open leaves the attribute stale, confusing screen reader users about the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3422: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The code declares a focus trap variable and documents intent to use it, but the open function never instantiates or activates the trap. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the underlying page, losing context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2052: Add the following two lines after `_lockScroll();`:
    `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
    `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric state where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function emits a custom event but does not announce the "Navigation menu closed" status to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. The open function correctly announces the state change, but closing the menu leaves screen reader users unaware of the context change.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in an incorrect accessible state when the dropdown opens.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function updates the DOM to show the dropdown panel but does not announce the expansion to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that a menu has opened dynamically.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel and updates aria-expanded to "false" but does not announce the collapse to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that a menu has closed dynamically.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function adds the open class and animates the panel height but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to report the accordion item as collapsed even when it is visually expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function emits a custom event but does not announce the "answer collapsed" status to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. The open function correctly announces the state change, but closing an answer leaves screen reader users unaware of the context change.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. The corresponding markValid function removes aria-invalid, creating a state where the invalid attribute is never applied, preventing assistive technology from identifying the field as erroneous.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._onViewToggle interaction lacks role and keyboard enforcement for toggle elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _onViewToggle handler processes clicks on [data-view-mode] elements without enforcing role="button" or tabindex="0" in JavaScript. If the HTML uses non-native elements for these view toggles, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users and lack the correct semantic role for the aria-pressed state being applied.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` and before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu panel does not update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true", violating the ARIA disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ accordion item updates the visual state but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so screen readers do not announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` and before the call to `animateHeight()`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but omits setting `aria-invalid="true"`, preventing screen readers from identifying the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the call to `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() omits live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation updates the visual state and emits a custom event but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to screen readers via the live region, unlike the `open()` function which provides an announcement.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes omit live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not announce the state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether the menu is now visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `_openInstance`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before the `emit()` call. In `_closeInstance`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent actions omit live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Accepting or declining cookies hides the banner but does not announce the action result (e.g., "Cookies accepted" or "Cookies declined") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `_onAccept`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiesAccepted || 'Cookies accepted');` before `_hideBanner();`. In `_onDecline`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiesDeclined || 'Cookies declined');` before `_hideBanner();`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are given role="button" and tabindex="0" but the keydown handler only supports arrow navigation; it does not handle Enter or Space to toggle the panel, making the accordion unusable for keyboard-only users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not trap focus
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating a focus trap, allowing keyboard focus to move outside the drawer to background elements, violating modal dialog focus containment requirements.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu outside-click close loses focus
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown via outside click calls _closeInstance without the returnFocus parameter, causing focus to be lost instead of returning to the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside dropdownMenu._onDocumentClick, change `_closeAll();` to:
    _instances.forEach(inst => { if (inst.open) _closeInstance(inst, true); });

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs lack keyboard activation handlers
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs use roving tabindex with activateOnFocus:false but the keydown handler does not support Enter/Space activation; if the HTML elements are not native buttons, keyboard users cannot activate tabs.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not manage aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the drawer visibility using CSS classes (addClass/removeClass) without updating aria-hidden. Since the element remains in the DOM, it must be removed from the accessibility tree when hidden to prevent screen reader access to invisible content.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This creates a state mismatch where the panel is visible but the trigger indicates it is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open function does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function sets the internal state to open and shows recent queries but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input. Screen readers relying on the combobox pattern will not know the suggestion list is expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel visually but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the answer is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed on tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function toggles the visual active class on filter tabs but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen reader users will not know which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid upon focus.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the function adds error classes and links the error message via aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (_renderResult): In the `if (result.valid)` block, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.
  Line (_renderResult): In the `else` block (invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before the existing srUtils.markInvalid call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set the initial aria-expanded state on the trigger button. If the navigation starts in a closed state, aria-expanded should be set to "false" so screen readers know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (init): After `_trigger  = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add:
  `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product cards are updated and the count badge is refreshed, but the change in result count (e.g., "Showing 5 products") is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (applyFilters): Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
  `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the open function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function omits the corresponding "true" state, leaving screen readers unaware that the item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (open): After `item.open = true;`, add:
  `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices are updated visually with animation, but the new price information is not announced to screen readers, leaving blind users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (setPeriod): After `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add:
  `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that expands and collapses a suggestion list, but the init function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the input element when the page loads.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (init): After `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add:
  `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm init missing aria-live on dynamically created status element
issuesFound.explanation: The status element is created with aria-live, but when it is hidden via `setAttribute('hidden', '')` in _showSuccess or _showError, it may not be announced properly in some screen readers because the element is hidden. However, the main issue is that the error status in _showError sets role='alert' but the success status in _showSuccess does not ensure it is assertive or polite. More critically, the _showError function sets `role: 'alert'` on the status element, but the _showSuccess function does not set `role: 'status'` explicitly (it inherits from creation). This is inconsistent.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (_showSuccess): Add `_statusEl.setAttribute('role', 'status');` after removing the hidden attribute.
  Line (_showError): Ensure `_statusEl.setAttribute('role', 'alert');` is set (already present, verify it remains).

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons that show the active filter state, but the function only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (_syncTabAriaStates): Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add:
  `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: Although the module declares a focus trap variable (_trap), the open function never activates it. When the navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the overlay to background content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (open): After `_lockScroll();`, add:
  `if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); }`
  `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: contactForm _renderErrorSummary missing programmatic focus management for error list links
issuesFound.explanation: The error summary is created and focused, but the links inside it point to field IDs that may not exist if the fields don't have IDs. The code uses `srUtils.ensureId(field, 'contact-field')` which generates IDs, but the href uses `#${srUtils.ensureId(field, 'contact-field')}` which is correct. However, after focusing the summary, the focus should ideally move to the first invalid field when the user activates the link. The current implementation relies on standard anchor link behavior, but ensure the fields actually have the IDs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (_renderErrorSummary): Ensure the href construction uses the same ID generation as the field ID assignment:
  `const fieldId = srUtils.ensureId(field, 'contact-field');`
  `const link = createElement('a', { href: `#${fieldId}` });`
  (Code already appears correct, but verify consistency).

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone missing aria-controls reference to input
issuesFound.explanation: The upload zone is given role="button" and tabindex="0", but it does not explicitly establish a programmatic relationship with the file input it controls via aria-controls, which would help screen readers understand the relationship between the zone and the input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (bind): After `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', ...);`, add:
  `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(inputEl, 'upload-input'));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via the liveRegion utility. Screen reader users are not notified that the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle dropdown panels but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Submenu opened") via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize a dropdown has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1962: Inside _openInstance(), add liveRegion.announce('Submenu opened'); Line 1982: Inside _closeInstance(), add liveRegion.announce('Submenu closed');

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the setPeriod() function updates price displays with animation but does not announce the price change or billing period switch via a live region. Screen reader users are unaware prices have changed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() utility function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to "false"). The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1252: Inside markValid(), add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); after clearing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed or aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles visual CSS classes for active filter tabs but does not set aria-pressed="true" or aria-selected="true" on the active button, nor does it remove these attributes from inactive buttons. Screen readers cannot identify which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3524: Inside _syncTabAriaStates(), add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); or setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive)); for each tab button.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips missing live region announcement on removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a user clicks a filter chip to remove it, the _onChipClick() handler updates the filter state and re-renders the chips, but does not announce via a live region that the filter has been removed. Screen readers do not confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3756: Inside the switch cases for removing filters, add liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed`); before or after calling productFilter methods.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply() function validates and applies discount codes, updating the price display, but only shows visual feedback in _statusEl. It does not announce the success or error message via a live region. Screen readers do not hear the discount confirmation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4856: Replace the visual-only status update with liveRegion.announce(result.message); for both success and error cases.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes the 'is-open' class from the _drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true" (or ensure the drawer has display:none via CSS). If CSS only moves the drawer off-screen, screen readers can still navigate to it. The code explicitly manages aria-hidden for the overlay but not the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1884: Inside close(), add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); Line 1856: Inside open(), add removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for createFocusTrap but never initializes it (remains null). Consequently, when the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the modal overlay into the underlying page content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: Inside init(), after _drawer is assigned, add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); Line 1856: Inside open(), add _trap.activate(); Line 1884: Inside close(), add _trap.deactivate();

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error, not just that it has a description.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". This leaves the field in a permanently invalid state for assistive technology even after the user corrects the error.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() does not update aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle active visual states (CSS classes) to indicate the selected category, but they do not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2857: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers missing Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ triggers are not native button elements (assigned role="button" and tabindex="0"), the click handler is attached to the container but there is no keydown handler for Enter or Space keys to activate the toggle, making the accordion unusable for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3580: Add cases for `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` in the _onKeydown handler that call the same toggle logic as the click handler.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2370: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function within the `mobileNav` module hides the navigation drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2395: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module reveals a modal-like drawer but does not activate a focus trap (the `_trap` variable is declared but never initialized or activated). Keyboard focus can escape the drawer, violating WCAG 2.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2370: function open(announceToSR) { ... _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate(); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module changes visible state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2370: function open(announceToSR) { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function within the `mobileNav` module changes visible state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2395: function close(announceToSR) { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register function does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function within the `dropdownMenu` module sets `aria-haspopup="true"` on the trigger but fails to initialize `aria-expanded="false"`. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2730: function register(trigger, panel) { ... setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module reveals the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2635: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module hides the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module changes visible state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that a dropdown has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2635: function _openInstance(inst) { ... liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function within the `dropdownMenu` module changes visible state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that a dropdown has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); ... }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open` function within the `siteSearch` module reveals the suggestion listbox (combobox pattern) but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `_inputEl`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: function _open() { ... setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_close` function within the `siteSearch` module hides the suggestion listbox but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the `_inputEl`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: function _close() { ... setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. The `close` function sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3655: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `faqAccordion` module changes visible state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that an FAQ answer has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3655: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question)); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function within the `faqAccordion` module changes visible state but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that an FAQ answer has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3685: function close(item, announceToSR) { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question)); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function in `srUtils` links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1665: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid function does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` function in `srUtils` removes the error description but fails to remove or reset `aria-invalid` to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: function markValid(field, errorEl) { ... field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); ... }

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function within the `pricing` module updates visible prices based on billing period but does not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3335: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters function does not announce results

issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: function applyFilters() { ... liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); ... }

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the markInvalid() function only updates aria-describedby to link to the error message, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the open() and close() functions, which add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer. However, neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) to reflect the expanded or collapsed state. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open(): Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after setting _open = true;. Line mobileNav.close(): Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after setting _open = false;.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product grid updates and the count badge changes, but there is no announcement via the live region to inform screen reader users how many products are now displayed or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance() and _closeInstance(), but fails to set aria-expanded="true" when opening or aria-expanded="false" when closing on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Although aria-haspopup is set during registration, the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after inst.open = true;. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after inst.open = false;.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually, but the change is not announced to screen reader users via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): After _updatePlanCard(plan, period); (inside the forEach), add a live region announcement: liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion listbox. The _open() and _close() functions manage the visibility state but do not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl) to indicate whether the suggestions are currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch._open(): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after _searchState.open = true;. Line siteSearch._close(): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after _searchState.open = false;.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the panel is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register(): After setting panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid() function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (by setting it to "false" or removing it). The field may still be reported as invalid by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid(): Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); after clearing the aria-describedby attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs lack aria-pressed or aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons use _syncTabAriaStates() to update visual active states, but the function does not set aria-pressed (for toggle buttons) or aria-selected (for tab semantics) to communicate the active filter to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(): Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); or setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after determining isActive.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button starts in a closed state when the page loads, but the init() function does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. Assistive technologies will not know the initial state of the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init(): After registering the trigger element (_trigger), add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the page loads and the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init(): After calling suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);, add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the open() and close() functions do not announce the state change via the live region, despite receiving an announceToSR parameter. Screen reader users are not informed when content is revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): Add if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question)); before the emit call. Line faqAccordion.close(): Add if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question)); before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid (markInvalid)
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference to the error element but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state, not just that it has a description.

issuesFound.title: Form validation reset not clearing aria-invalid (markValid)
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. This leaves the field in a permanently invalid state in the accessibility tree even after the user corrects the error.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions show and hide the panel but do not toggle aria-expanded on the inst.trigger button. Users cannot perceive the state change of the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function initializes the panel as hidden but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item to open and removes hidden from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and siteSearch._close functions show and hide the suggestion listbox but do not toggle aria-expanded on the _inputEl. Combobox patterns require this attribute to indicate if the popup is open.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function toggles the visual checked class on the toggle track but does not update aria-checked on the _toggleEl (which is assigned role="switch"). Switch controls require aria-checked to convey state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function accepts an announceToSR argument but does not use the liveRegion module to announce the menu state change. Users are not informed when the navigation opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: Product filter result count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible count badge and DOM but does not announce the new result count to an aria-live region. Users are unaware that the product list has been filtered.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and emits a custom event but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to an aria-live region.

issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection section contains a heading h1#hero-heading but the section element lacks aria-labelledby. The landmark region is not properly named for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 872: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard article contains a heading h3#headingId but the article element lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks or articles cannot identify the plan.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1203: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component uses a nav element without an aria-label. If multiple navs exist on the page, users cannot distinguish this navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 558: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component uses a nav element without an aria-label. Since MobileNav also exists, distinct labels are required to distinguish the navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 731: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in DesktopNav apply a visual current class but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed which page is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 749: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology. It may be announced as "asterisk" or "star" unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 532: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not apply aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element. Users are not informed that the button is in a busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 483: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button spinner span missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner span rendered inside the Button during loading is not hidden from assistive technology. It serves no semantic purpose and adds noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 495: <span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />

issuesFound.title: StarRating inner star spans missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside StarRating are decorative but lack aria-hidden. The container has an aria-label, so the children should be hidden to prevent verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned list in IntegrationsBar used for visual looping lacks aria-hidden. Screen readers may read the duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 989: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This prevents assistive technology from correctly identifying the modal context.
issuesFound.fix:
